meta.name: Departure Mono / Retro-Future Sci-Fi Technical Design System
meta.genre: Retro-futurist sci-fi instrumentation + technical-editorial + travel-document collage
meta.coreIdea: A single monospaced pixel typeface applied uniformly across wildly different contexts (arcade game, spacecraft HUD, code terminal, type specimen, printed travel documents), unified by strict monochrome-plus-one-accent color rules, sharp corners, hairline rules, and dense micro-label annotation.

typeface.characterStyle: visible stair-step pixel curves, fixed glyph width, low-res/8-bit feel
typeface.usageRule: Used for 100% of text in every composition — headlines, labels, body, tables, code. No secondary typeface is ever introduced.

caseConventions.microLabels/captions: UPPERCASE, 10-12px, wide letter-spacing (~0.1-0.2em), placed directly above the element they describe
caseConventions.data/tables: sentence or upper case depending on field, strict column alignment

scale.context: annotation tags above nearly every block/component
tabular numeric/text data, monospace alignment critical

specialTreatments: Display wordmarks sometimes substitute numerals for visually similar letters (e.g., 7 for T, 3 for E) for a glitch/technical flavor.
Highlighted single characters (e.g., one letter in an alphabet grid) get an accent-color background block to demonstrate/showcase state.

accent.usage: single functional accent — active states, key data points, HUD lines, highlighted keywords, ball/paddle in game UI
ticket/boarding-pass accent panels, highlight-marker text overlays

globalRules: Exactly one accent color per composition — never mix amber and sage in the same screen.
Dark screens = near-black base + amber accent = 'instrument panel' feel.
Light screens = off-white/paper base + desaturated secondary (kraft tan or sage green) = 'printed ephemera' feel.
Color is always functional (status, emphasis, active/highlighted data), never purely decorative.

layoutPatterns.structure: Centered focal graphic (brick formation / radar reticle) on vertical axis, small labeled stat readouts flanking left and right, footer logo lockup bottom-left, fine-print bottom-right.

layoutPatterns.pattern: Split panel: technical backdrop + floating paper card
layoutPatterns.structure: Dark code/terminal panel with syntax-highlighted keywords on the left; a lighter rectangular 'document' card overlaid on top with soft shadow, containing markdown-style headers and a bordered data table.
Page divided into independent labeled data blocks (metrics table, keyboard-layout diagram, glyph-metrics diagram with measurement lines, full character grid) — each with its own micro-label caption.

Central circular reticle/crosshair with dashed orbit rings; vertical tick-mark axis of numbers beside it; left/right vertical stacks of labeled percentage gauge bars; top-center title flanked by tick marks; small icon buttons along bottom.
Vertical rhythm of [micro-label caption] + [type sample] pairs at decreasing size, generous whitespace, strict left alignment, no visual dividers needed between sections.

2-4 rectangular 'documents' of differing paper colors (white thermal receipt, dark boarding pass, kraft evidence card) overlapped at slight offsets/rotation with soft drop shadows, perforated/dashed tear-edges on ticket-style pieces.

Hairline horizontal rules between rows, monospace column alignment, header row same weight as body (no bold), small sort-direction arrow icon in one header cell, light line-art illustration anchoring the top of the page.

Large pixel wordmark top-left with highlight block behind it, version tag beside it, uppercase nav links top-right with small leading icons; below, a loosely arranged mixed-media collage: business letter, kraft evidence card with QR code + paperclip, dark app-card mockups, dotted orbit line-art, dot-grid decorative pattern.

componentLibrary.navLinks: Text-only, uppercase, small leading icon (arrow/heart/chevron), no background fill.
componentLibrary.badgesAndLockups: Rectangular bordered box (often solid fill behind text) containing an uppercase wordmark or label — used for logos and status pills.
componentLibrary.gauges/progressBars: Horizontal bar, segmented or solid fill, numeric/percentage label right-aligned; repeated in vertical stacks for multi-stat readouts (fuel, pressure, etc).
componentLibrary.tables: 1px hairline borders/rules only, no bold header differentiation, monospace numeric alignment, optional sort icon.
componentLibrary.documentCards: Rectangular 'paper' card, soft low-opacity drop shadow, optional 1-3° rotation, dashed/perforated edge when representing tickets or receipts.
componentLibrary.annotationDots: Small accent-colored dot markers paired with a coordinate/number micro-label — used on schematics, diagrams, and highlighted data.
componentLibrary.dashedLines: Used two ways: (1) perforation/tear lines on ticket-style cards, (2) orbital/trajectory paths in decorative line-art.
componentLibrary.lineArtIllustrations: Thin 1px monochrome technical/schematic drawings — blueprint circles, pen nibs, orbit paths, barcodes, QR codes — rendered in the muted neutral or accent color.
componentLibrary.reticle/crosshair: Circular radar-style target graphic with center dot and axis ticks, used in HUD contexts.
componentLibrary.highlightMarker: Flat accent-color rectangle behind a run of text, mimicking a highlighter pen, used in printed-letter mockups.

motifsAndWorldbuilding.fictionalUniverse: A consistent retro-future space-travel/research world is used as placeholder content across all pieces rather than generic filler text: a fictional airline ('Kärmán Spacelines'), destinations ('Kuiper Alpha', 'Mars Landing', 'New Tokyo', 'Europa 1', 'Zvezda Moonbase'), research organizations ('Mercury Research Labs', a Farnsworth-named research institute), mission logs, boarding passes, and diner receipts.
visualReferences: retro sci-fi spacecraft instrument panels (amber monochrome HUDs)
physical travel ephemera — boarding passes, luggage tags, thermal receipts, typed business letters
consistentDevices: One monospace/pixel typeface for all text, no exceptions
Exactly one accent color per composition
Tiny uppercase micro-label captions attached to nearly every discrete element, functioning like technical spec annotations
Real-world document formats reproduced with authentic details (barcodes, QR codes, perforation, letterhead, itemized receipts)

spacingAndGrid.borderWeight: 1px hairline throughout; low-contrast gray in neutral contexts, accent color when indicating active/highlighted structure
spacingAndGrid.cornerRadius: 0px — the entire system is sharp-cornered/rectangular; no rounded corners anywhere
spacingAndGrid.whitespaceDensity: generous/airy in light-mode editorial layouts; denser and more instrument-like in dark-mode HUD/game layouts

effectsAndTexture.shadows: Soft, low-opacity drop shadows reserved exclusively for elements meant to read as physical paper/cards stacked on a surface.
effectsAndTexture.gradientsOrGlows: None — all fields are flat color; no gradients, no blur/glow effects.
effectsAndTexture.pixelation: Typography (and some icon glyphs, e.g. the game's blocks) sits on a visible pixel grid; all other graphic elements (rules, tables, line-art, shadows) are rendered as clean vector shapes for contrast.

aiReplicationGuidelines: Set the base font-family to a monospaced/bitmap typeface (Departure Mono or a close substitute) for ALL text in the design — headlines, labels, body, and tables alike.

Choose exactly one mode per screen: dark-base + amber accent (technical/HUD/game contexts) OR light-base + neutral-with-one-secondary-accent (editorial/print/travel-document contexts). Never combine both accent colors in one composition.
Caption components with small (10-12px) uppercase, wide-tracked micro-labels placed directly above them — this is the system's signature 'technical documentation' texture.
Keep every corner at 0px radius. Use 1px hairline borders/rules for structure in flat UI; reserve soft drop shadows only for elements meant to look like loose physical paper.
For dashboard/HUD screens: build a symmetric layout anchored on a central circular reticle/gauge, flanked by vertical stacks of labeled percentage bars and a tick-marked numeric axis.

For document/collage layouts: overlap 2-4 rectangular 'paper' elements of different tones (white, dark, kraft, sage) at slight rotation/offset with soft shadows, as if scattered on a desk or evidence board.

Fill placeholder content with the established sci-fi travel/research fictional universe (spacelines, exoplanet destinations, mission logs, research institutes) instead of generic lorem ipsum, to preserve tone.
Use color strictly functionally: accent = active/highlighted/key data only. Everything else stays neutral gray/black/white.
Style tables with monospace column alignment, hairline row dividers, non-bold headers, and at most one small sort/direction icon — avoid heavy table chrome.
